introduction.text: You know the Golden Rules. Now, let's look at the tricky pairs: 2s, 3s, 4s, 6s, 7s, and 9s. The rule here is context. Look at MY card. If I am weak, you Split to attack. If I am strong, do not Split – just play it like a normal hand.

sequence.text: You have two 9s. Total 18. Dealer has a 7. Think! If dealer has 10 underneath, he has 17. Your 18 beats 17. Why risk splitting? Just Take the money. STAND.

finish.text: Smart. I had 17. You had 18. Easy win without the stress of splitting.

sequence.text: Now I have a 6 (Weak). You have 18. 18 is good, but winning TWO bets is better. Split the 9s to punish my weak card.
Hand 1 is 19 (Stand). Hand 2 is 11. Look! 11 vs 6. You know what to do with the second hand. DOUBLE!

finish.text: Jackpot! Hand 1 won (19 vs bust). Hand 2 hit 21. You maximized your profit perfectly.

scenarios.title: Small Pairs vs Weak Dealer (2,2 vs 5)

sequence.text: Pair of 2s. Total 4. That's a terrible starting number. But I have a 5. Split them! Turn one bad hand (4) into two decent starts.

finish.text: I busted. Since you split, you win twice. If you had just hit the 4, you would only win once.

scenarios.title: Small Pairs vs Strong Dealer (6,6 vs 8)
scenarios.goal: Teach HITTING (not splitting) pairs when dealer is strong.

sequence.text: Two 6s. Total 12. Dealer has 8. DANGER. If you split, you start two hands with 6 against an 8. You will likely lose both. Just Hit the 12 like a normal Hard Hand.

finish.text: Good decision. You got 19 and beat my 18. If you had split, you would have been crushed.

scenarios.title: The 4,4 Exception (4,4 vs 3)
scenarios.goal: Teach that 4,4 is usually a Hit (unless table says otherwise).

sequence.text: Two 4s. Total 8. Should we split? No. 4 is a terrible starting card. 8 is a decent starting number (you can't bust). Don't split 4s here. Just Hit.

finish.text: Perfect sequence. Hit 4s, get 11, Double, Win.

conclusion.text: Congratulations! You have finished the Basic Strategy Course. You know every button and every decision. But... is it possible to know what card comes next? In the Final Lesson, we will peek behind the curtain. Lesson 10: Card Counting.
